refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with the acceptance criterion requiring 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit audit and change control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability from worksite input to derived target is documented and queryable; (e) history is immutable and tamper-evident.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle requirements, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record baseline performance targets with full traceability metadata (source, timestamp, operator, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain immutable audit trail of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface to retrieve current and historical performance targets with traceability.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance management service that ingests mission context via API, runs derivation algorithms, persists baselines and audit logs in a dedicated database, and exposes query/reporting endpoints.
DP-B: Distributed derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with a shared audit log service for history management.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with system-assisted validation and automated audit logging only.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance management service with mission-context ingestion API, algorithmic derivation engine, baseline persistence, and immutable audit log repository. This service shall expose Tasking interface for mission input, Telemetry interface for operational feedback, and a dedicated reporting interface for performance target queries.

potential_sources: DP-A couples to mission context input (Tasking interface) and operational telemetry (Telemetry interface), but these are standard system interfaces with well-defined contracts.
DP-A is decoupled from Vehicle and Teleoperation implementation details; it operates on abstracted mission and telemetry data.
Audit log persistence is self-contained; no tight coupling to external storage systems if database is treated as a service dependency.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it centralizes derivation logic and audit responsibility in a single service boundary, reducing diffusion of performance management concerns across multiple modules. Coupling to Tasking and Telemetry is acceptable because these are system-level interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface in a structured, parseable format.
Assumption: Derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated offline.
Risk: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation may produce invalid targets; mitigation: validation rules and fallback defaults.
Risk: High-frequency updates to performance targets could overwhelm audit log; mitigation: batch logging and archival strategy.
Risk: Safety criticality requires that audit log is tamper-proof and compliant with regulatory standards; mitigation: cryptographic signing and immutable storage.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) derivation algorithm specification document, (2) audit log schema and retention policy, (3) API contract for Tasking and Telemetry interfaces, (4) fallback behavior if mission context is unavailable.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module shall contain the performance management service, including mission-context ingestion, derivation algorithms, baseline persistence, audit log repository, and reporting interface. This module owns the end-to-end responsibility for CR-NGHE-005.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context (terrain, payload, cycle requirements, safety constraints) from Site Manager or planning system. Used to trigger performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational telemetry (actual cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Used for baseline validation and performance tracking.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits alerts if derived performance targets conflict with safety constraints or if historical baselines indicate degradation. Directed to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for mission context ingestion and telemetry reception. May be used for remote audit log synchronization if NGHE Solution spans multiple sites.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container and the natural home for performance management. All derivation logic, baseline persistence, and audit responsibility are contained. Minimal diffusion: only external interface dependencies (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are necessary system-level contracts.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing missions and reporting telemetry, not for deriving or managing performance requirements. Placing derivation logic in Vehicle would couple performance management to vehicle-specific implementation and diffuse audit responsibility across multiple vehicle instances.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and control, not for performance requirement derivation. Placing derivation logic here would couple performance management to tele-operator workflows and create diffusion if multiple tele-operators or vehicles are involved.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alerts, not for performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services may consume SafetyAlert outputs from performance management, it should not own the derivation logic itself. Containment score reflects potential for safety-related validation, but primary responsibility belongs to NGHE Solution.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because: (1) it is the system-level container responsible for end-to-end mission management, (2) performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that must be centralized to ensure consistency and auditability, (3) all external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are system-level contracts, (4) containment score of 95 reflects minimal diffusion and high cohesion.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is decomposed into sub-modules (e.g., mission planner, performance manager, safety coordinator), ownership of performance management must be explicitly assigned to one sub-module to avoid diffusion.
Risk: If multiple instances of NGHE Solution exist (e.g., for different worksites), audit log synchronization and baseline consistency must be managed at a higher level.
Risk: If Tasking or Telemetry interfaces are not stable or well-defined, performance management service may become tightly coupled to upstream changes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) explicit specification of NGHE Solution sub-module structure if further decomposition is planned, (2) definition of audit log synchronization strategy if multiple NGHE Solution instances exist, (3) formal specification of Tasking and Telemetry interface contracts to minimize coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements with traceability and audit trail. Selected DP-A (centralized performance management service) directly addresses all FR elements: (1) mission-context ingestion satisfies 'derive from worksite mission context', (2) baseline persistence satisfies 'record', (3) audit log satisfies 'manage history', (4) reporting interface satisfies 'traceable'. No independence violations detected; FR and DP are well-aligned.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-A is assigned to a single IM boundary (NGHE Solution), eliminating the risk of performance management logic being scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.
Interface dependencies (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are system-level contracts, not implementation details, so coupling is acceptable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module has a containment score of 95 and diffusion score of 5, indicating excellent fit. All derivation algorithms, baseline persistence, and audit responsibility are contained within a single module boundary. External interfaces are minimal and well-defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible within NGHE Solution IM boundary: (1) performance target derivation can be tested with synthetic mission contexts, (2) baseline recording can be verified via database inspection, (3) audit trail can be validated by checking log entries for completeness and immutability, (4) traceability can be verified by querying the system for source-to-target mappings, (5) history management can be tested by simulating updates and verifying audit log entries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the entire CR-NGHE-005 requirement. No sub-allocation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services is needed at this level. If NGHE Solution is further decomposed, sub-allocations will be made at the next level.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the Tasking interface contract to specify mission context schema (terrain, payload, cycle requirements, safety constraints).
Formalize the Telemetry interface contract to specify operational data schema (cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics).
Define audit log schema, retention policy, and tamper-proofing mechanism (e.g., cryptographic signing).
Specify fallback behavior if mission context is incomplete or unavailable.
Define performance target derivation algorithm specification document with validation rules.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined, a sound DP has been selected, and a correct IM assignment has been made to NGHE Solution. All gates pass. However, the refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that require further functional decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will detail the derivation algorithms, baseline persistence mechanism, audit log structure, and reporting interface. This is a normal and healthy progression in hierarchical functional decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle requirements, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record baseline performance targets with full traceability metadata (source, timestamp, operator, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain immutable audit trail of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface to retrieve current and historical performance targets with traceability.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface in a structured, parseable format.
Derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated offline.
NGHE Solution is the appropriate system-level container for performance management.
Audit log persistence can be implemented with standard database technology.
Safety criticality can be addressed through audit trail immutability and tamper-proofing.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation may produce invalid targets.
High-frequency updates to performance targets could overwhelm audit log.
Audit log must be tamper-proof and compliant with regulatory standards.
If NGHE Solution is decomposed into sub-modules, ownership of performance management must be explicitly assigned.
If multiple instances of NGHE Solution exist, audit log synchronization and baseline consistency must be managed.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of Tasking interface contract (mission context schema).
Formal specification of Telemetry interface contract (operational data schema).
Audit log schema and retention policy document.
Performance target derivation algorithm specification with validation rules.
Fallback behavior specification for incomplete mission context.
Regulatory compliance assessment for audit trail requirements.
Test plan for performance target derivation, baseline recording, and audit trail management.
